methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
First Light Fusion is a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Nicholas Hawker and Yiannis Ventikos, having raised $100M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector contains 11 identifiable peers (3 public, 8 private), including companies such as Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy at various development stages.

The stage-progression method is applicable to this valuation. Using the $100M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 1.7x multiplier for Series C progression yields an implied valuation of $170M. The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy sector—only two adjacent-stage peers were identified, below the three-company threshold required for statistical reliability. A sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable bias given the heterogeneous development stages and technology approaches across nuclear energy peers.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a median valuation of $170M, with a range of $144M (low) to $195M (high) reflecting uncertainty bands around stage progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiple for First Light's specific technology maturity relative to peer trajectories, the absence of direct same-stage comparables for calibration, and burn rate or capital requirements not captured in this data. A broader peer set or updated comparable valuations would strengthen confidence in this range.
